data.title: Musk supports putting US Treasury transactions on-chain
data.description: According to Watcher.Guru on the X platform, Musk supports putting U.S. Treasury transactions on the blockchain for full transparency.

data.title: Elon Musk Advocates For Blockchain Transparency In U.S. Treasury Transactions
data.description: According to Odaily, Elon Musk has expressed support for placing U.S. Treasury transactions on the blockchain to achieve complete transparency. This statement was shared by Watcher.Guru on the X platform, highlighting Musk's advocacy for leveraging blockchain technology to enhance financial transparency.

data.title: Strategy reports GAAP loss per share of $3.03 in Q4 2024
data.description: Bitcoin Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) reported a net loss of $3.03 per share in the fourth quarter of 2024, compared with earnings of $0.50 per share in the same period last year. The loss was caused by the company's impairment charge on its Bitcoin holdings. As of December 31, 2024, the Bitcoin holdings were 447,470, while the market expected 425,149. The company recently ended a series of Bitcoin purchases and currently holds 471,107 Bitcoins, worth about $45 billion. The U.S. Financial Stability Accounting Board (FASB) implemented a new fair value accounting rule for companies holding digital assets last year. The use of the rule is voluntary before the end of 2024, but it will be mandatory starting in the first quarter of this year.

data.title: Strategy announces 2025 annual “BTC revenue” target of $10 billion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bitcoin finance company Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) announced a 2025 "BTC revenue" target of $10 billion, and revised the 2025 BTC annual yield target to at least 15%. The company's "BTC yield" KPI reached 74.3% in fiscal 2024 and 2.9% in the first quarter of 2025. Phong Le, president and CEO of the company, said: "We have completed $20 billion of our $42 billion capital plan, significantly ahead of our original schedule, while leading the digital transformation of financial market capital. Looking ahead to the remainder of 2025, we are ready to further enhance shareholder value by leveraging the strong support of institutional and retail investors for our strategic plans."

data.title: Strategy Reports Q4 Loss Due To Bitcoin Impairment
data.description: According to Odaily, Strategy, formerly known as MicroStrategy, reported a net loss of $3.03 per share for the fourth quarter of 2024, contrasting with earnings of $0.50 per share in the same period the previous year. The loss was attributed to impairment charges on the company's Bitcoin holdings. As of December 31, 2024, Strategy held 447,470 Bitcoins, surpassing market expectations of 425,149. Recently, the company completed a series of Bitcoin purchases, bringing its total holdings to 471,107 Bitcoins, valued at approximately $45 billion. The Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) in the United States introduced a new fair value accounting rule for companies holding digital assets last year. While its application was voluntary until the end of 2024, it became mandatory starting the first quarter of this year.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Highlights Focus on 10-Year Treasury Yield
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent stated that he and U.S. President Donald Trump are primarily focused on the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield. Following significant interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, the yield on the 10-year Treasury has increased. Besent noted that President Trump has not called for the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates.

data.title: The earnings growth of the seven major US stocks slowed down, and the rise of the S&P 500 index was threatened
data.description: The seven largest companies in the U.S. have boosted earnings growth and stock returns for the S&P 500, accounting for about a third of the benchmark index. More than half of the index's gains over the past two years have come from these companies, but their profit growth is slowing as spending grows. In terms of forward price-to-earnings ratios, the Big Seven are 40% premium to the S&P 500 as a whole, so these concerns pose a threat to their high valuations. This premium level is already narrowing, below the peak of 70% in 2023. Industry research data shows that the year-on-year growth rate of earnings of these giants peaked at the end of 2023 and is expected to slow for the fifth consecutive quarter. Kristian Heugh, manager of the Morgan Stanley Global Opportunities Fund, said the year-on-year growth in earnings of the Big Seven is "substantially" declining, while other components of the S&P 500 are improving. (Jinshi)

data.title: Atlanta Fed GDPNow model predicts that the US GDP growth rate in the first quarter of 2025 will be 2.9%
data.description: The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model expects U.S. GDP growth to be 2.9% in the first quarter of 2025, compared with a previous forecast of 3.9%.

data.title: Atlanta Fed GDPNow Model Adjusts U.S. Q1 2025 GDP Growth Forecast
data.description: According to Odaily, the Atlanta Federal Reserve's GDPNow model has revised its forecast for the United States' first-quarter GDP growth in 2025. The updated projection estimates a growth rate of 2.9%, down from the previous forecast of 3.9%.

data.title: The U.S. FDIC is re-evaluating its regulation of cryptocurrency activities, and the original regulatory warning has been deleted
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to crypto KOL MartyParty, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) announced today that it is actively re-evaluating its regulatory approach to cryptocurrency-related activities. This includes replacing Financial Institution Letter (FIL) 16-2022 and providing a path for institutions to participate in cryptocurrency and blockchain-related activities while adhering to the principles of security and soundness. 
This information was emphasized in a statement by Acting Chairman Travis Hill, indicating that the FDIC has changed in regulating banks involved in cryptocurrency activities. This means: banks can custody customers' cryptocurrencies and they will be insured by the FDIC. The Biden administration's Choke Point FIL 16-2022 letter has been withdrawn and replaced. 
MartyParty commented: The original FIL-16-2022 letter link has been deleted and displays a 404 error. The FDIC is deleting all evidence related to its politically motivated policy against cryptocurrency.
